text.title: RACI Matrix Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the RACI Matrix methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each row MUST have exactly one Accountable (A); rows with zero or multiple A entries are rejected.
rule.rationale: multiple A's mean nobody is accountable in practice.

rule.statement: Each row MUST list >=1 Responsible (R); R MAY equal A but cannot be empty.
rule.rationale: R-empty rows describe 'nobody is doing the work'.

rule.statement: Consulted (C) implies two-way dialogue BEFORE action; Informed (I) implies one-way AFTER action; mixing them is rejected.
rule.rationale: C/I conflation degrades the matrix into a contact list.

rule.statement: Each named assignment MUST cite source (engagement charter, role-card, prior decision); un-sourced names are flagged 'verify-with-lead'.
rule.rationale: agent-supplied names are the #1 cause of 'I never agreed to this' in retros.

rule.statement: Each row SHOULD have <=8 stakeholders assigned (any of R/A/C/I); rows with more become unactionable.
rule.rationale: >8 stakeholders per task means the task is over-scoped or the matrix is wallpaper.

rule.statement: RACI MUST set a review cadence (monthly / quarterly) and the next review_due date.
rule.rationale: RACIs without review cadence drift from reality within 60-90 days.
